free_text: 26Al+p elastic and inelastic scattering reactions and
galactic abundances of 26Al
Sungkyunkwan University, Suwon
Experiment performed at the Holifield
Radioactive Ion Beam Facility (HRIBF) at Oak Ridge
National Laboratory
Polypropylene (CH2) target with thickness of 46 mu-
g/cm2
Protons scattered out of the target were
detected in the Silicon Detector Array (SIDAR),
consisting of six 1000 mu-m and two 500 mu-m YY1
silicon detectors manufactured by Micron
Semiconductors, Ltd.The array subtended laboratory
angles 18 - 41 degrees off the beam axis.
A gas-filled ionization chamber was placed
downstream of the target chamber to detect scattered
26Al particles in coincidence with protons scattered
into SIDAR.
Coincidence of scattered 26Al ions detected
in ionization chamber with protons scattered into
SIDAR array was required. Inverse kinematics was used
Pure radioactive 26Al beam was obtained by placing
sintered aluminium oxide (with 26Al) in the Cs-sputter
ion source on the RIB platform. Beam intensities of
approx. 2 x 10E+6 26Al/s were achieved
Compiled by S.H.
Data taken from fig. 2 of the reference
Waiting for data from author
Statistical uncertainties
